toolResult.summary: Removed 2 component(s), Added 1 component(s), Created 2 wire(s), Updated sketch

trace.text:
✅ **Done!** Buttons replaced with potentiometer.

### **Wiring Summary**
| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin Role |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| D2–D8 | 7-seg Segments (a–g via 220Ω resistors) | Display segments |
| A0 (D14) | POT_CONTROL | Analog input (signal) |
| 5V | POT_CONTROL | Power |
| GND | POT_CONTROL, 7-seg ground | Ground rail |

### **How It Works**
- **Potentiometer** on A0 reads 0–1023.
- **Mapping**: Divides by 102 to map to digits 0–9.
- **Display updates** only when digit changes (debounced every 100 ms).
- Smooth, no jumping—rotate the pot knob to see the counter change.

### **Power Budget**
- **A0 (analog input)**: <1 mA
- **D2–D8 (segment drivers)**: ~11 mA each × 7 segments = ~77 mA max
- **Potentiometer**: <1 mA
- **Total**: ~80 mA ✅ (well within 500 mA USB limit)
- **Safety**: All pins ≤ 20 mA single constraint met.

Done! Twist the pot to control the 0–9 display.
